channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Leilani Simon booked into Georgia prison, new mugshot released
item.description: Prosecutors said Leilani Simon killed her toddler son Quinton and threw his body in a dumpster in October 2022.
item.dc:creator: Molly Curley, NewsNation

item.title: Thousands of Lebanese return to their homes as Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire takes hold
item.description: TYRE, Lebanon (AP) — Thousands of Lebanese displaced by the war between Israel and Hezbollah militants returned home on Wednesday as a ceasefire took hold, driving cars stacked with personal belongings and defying warnings from Lebanese and Israeli troops to stay away from some areas.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, KAREEM CHEHAYEB and TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Meet the medical contrarians picked to lead health agencies under Trump and Kennedy
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has assembled a team of medical contrarians and health care critics to fulfill an agenda aimed at remaking how the federal government oversees medicines, health programs and nutrition. On Tuesday night, Trump nominated Dr.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Wendy Williams 'permanently incapacitated' as dementia progresses, guardian says
item.description: Wendy Williams, longtime host of the daytime talk show "The Wendy Williams Show," is "permanently incapacitated," according to her guardian.
item.dc:creator: Justyn Melrose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump nominees targeted in series of threats, spokesperson says
item.description: (The Hill) - Multiple nominees and appointees tapped to serve in President-elect Trump’s incoming administration were targeted with threats this week, a spokesperson for the transition said Wednesday, though it was unclear how many were affected.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Scammers using fake QR codes at parking lots: Tampa Police
item.description: Police are warning the community about a scam that targets people looking to use mobile payment methods in city parking lots.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Russia expels two German journalists in tit-for-tat retaliation, ministry says
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia's Foreign Ministry said Wednesday it has revoked the accreditation of two employees with the German ARD broadcaster and ordered them to leave the country in what it described as a retaliation to German authorities' move targeting two journalists from Russia's state TV.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Sleeping man saved from rolling off bridge by police officer, Good Samaritan in dramatic video
item.description: Dramatic new video has been released showing the moment a quick-thinking police officer and a Good Samaritan save a sleeping man from rolling off a bridge in Oklahoma.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 Americans held for years in China have been released, White House says
item.description: Three American citizens imprisoned for years by China have been released and are returning to the United States, the White House said Wednesday, announcing a rare diplomatic agreement with Beijing in the final months of the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: California man arrested for allegedly smuggling meth-caked clothing through LAX
item.description: Authorities found more than a dozen clothing items, including a cow pajama onesie, that were dried stiff and covered in a white residue.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Chow, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers call for progress on marijuana law before end of Biden administration
item.description: Progressive lawmakers are calling on President Biden to take additional steps on federal marijuana law before his term comes to an end in January. In a letter sent to Biden and Vice President Harris, Democratic Reps. Barbara Lee (Calif.), Earl Blumenauer (Ore.) and Ilhan Omar (Minn.) and Sens.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Oregon tribe has hunting and fishing rights restored under a long-sought court ruling
item.description: LINCOLN CITY, Ore. (AP) — Drumming made the floor vibrate and singing filled the conference room of the Chinook Winds Casino Resort in Lincoln City, on the Oregon coast, as hundreds in tribal regalia danced in a circle.
item.dc:creator: CLAIRE RUSH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Disney agrees to $43 million settlement in gender pay lawsuit
item.description: The agreement settles allegations initially made in a 2019 lawsuit filed by Disney employee LaRonda Rasmussen, who claimed she was getting paid less than her male counterparts in the same role.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China releases 3 Americans in prisoner swap
item.description: Mark Swidan, Kai Li and John Leung were freed after years of talks between U.S. and Chinese officials.
item.dc:creator: Katie Smith, NewsNation

item.title: Fired workers blamed for knocking over 120 headstones at Tennessee cemetery
item.description: More than 120 headstones at a Tennessee cemetery were knocked over by freshly fired employees, according to the cemetery's owner.
item.dc:creator: Shay Arthur, ABC 4

item.title: Zach Bryan pauses concert, calls out fan for throwing object on stage
item.description: A country music star whose 'argumentative' behavior has got him in hot water before, paused a recent concert after a fan appears to have thrown something onto stage.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: Liberal Nantucket reels from migrant crime wave as Biden spends Thanksgiving in rich friend’s mansion
item.description: President Biden and the first family arrived at Nantucket Island on Tuesday to continue their tradition of holding Thanksgiving on the island, despite a spate of migrant crime there.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Most expensive residential sale in Tennessee history closes at $42.5M
item.description: Tennessee's most expensive residential real estate sale just happened in Williamson county. Twin Rivers Farm and its more than 383 acres is now off the market after an unnamed buyer snagged it for $42.5 million.
item.dc:creator: Nickelle Smith, NewsNation

item.title: What dynamic pricing means for Black Friday shopping
item.description: If it feels like prices are on a roller coaster ride during the holidays, you’re not imagining things.
item.dc:creator: Amanda Barroso, ABC 4

item.title: Trump vows tariffs over immigration. What the numbers say about border crossings, drugs and crime
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a Monday evening announcement, President-elect Donald Trump railed against Mexico and Canada, accusing them of allowing thousands of people to enter the U.S.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA SANTANA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Thanksgiving Travel Latest: TSA braces for ‘busiest ever’ holiday
item.description: The Thanksgiving travel rush is expected to be bigger than ever this year. AAA predicts that nearly 80 million people in the U.S. will venture at least 50 miles (80 kilometers) from home between Tuesday and next Monday. Another round of wintry weather could complicate travel, though.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: HIV-positive organ transplants allowed under new rule
item.description: People with HIV will soon be permitted to receive transplanted kidneys and livers from donors who are also infected with the virus under a new rule approved by the Biden administration.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Drought is causing saltwater to creep up the Delaware River. Here's what's being done about it
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (AP) — Salty oceanwater is creeping up the Delaware River, the source for much of the drinking water for Philadelphia and millions of others, brought on by drought conditions and sea level rise, and prompting officials to tap reservoirs to push the unpotable tide back downstream.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CATALINI and BRITTANY PETERSON, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fugitive on FBI's 'Most Wanted Terrorists' list, accused of 2003 California bombings, arrested in Wales
item.description: An animal rights activist accused of two bombings in California in 2003 was arrested in Wales after more than 20 years on the run, the FBI says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sheriff’s defense in judge murder could depend on deposition in separate case: report
item.description: Shawn Stines' attorney reportedly said that his clients' deposition in a sex abuse lawsuit just before the shooting could play a "crucial role" in his defense.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Texas police officer fatally 'ambushed' in shootout with fleeing suspect: law enforcement
item.description: A police officer was fatally "ambushed" during a shootout with a fleeing suspect in Greenville, Texas, on Monday, according to local law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump threatens to impose sweeping tariffs on Mexico, Canada and China on first day
item.description: The tariffs, if implemented, could dramatically raise prices for American consumers on everything from gas to automobiles to agricultural products.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Menendez brothers' resentencing request hearing pushed back to January
item.description: The Menendez brothers have waiting nearly three decades, and now they'll have to wait a little longer to hear their fates.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, ABC 4

item.title: Biden proposes Medicare, Medicaid cover costly weight-loss drugs like like Wegovy or Ozempic
item.description: A bipartisan coalition of congressional members have lobbied for the drugs to be covered by Medicare, saying it could save the government from spending billions of dollars on treating chronic ailments that stem from obesity.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Remains of Pennsylvania teen at center of 1973 cold case identified as investigation continues
item.description: Ruth Elizabeth Brenneman was last seen leaving for school more than 50 years ago. Now, her remains have been identified as the investigation into her disappearance continues.
item.dc:creator: George Stockburger, ABC 4

item.title: Forecasts warn of possible winter storms across US during Thanksgiving week
item.description: WINDSOR, Calif. (AP) — Another round of wintry weather could complicate travel leading up to the Thanksgiving holiday, according to forecasts across the U.S., while California and Washington state continue to recover from storm damage and power outages.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Walmart executive says some prices might rise if Trump's tariffs take effect
item.description: A Walmart executive warned that President-elect Trump’s plans to raise tariffs on imports could drive the retail giant to increase the price of store items.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Bishop T.D. Jakes experiences 'health incident' during Sunday service
item.description: Bishop T.D. Jakes experienced a "slight health incident" while wrapping up his sermon at the Potter's House of Dallas on Sunday, the church confirmed in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, WGN 9

item.title: 20 cars stranded in Oregon snow after faulty GPS directions
item.description: Usually, when you turn to a GPS, you're trying not to get stranded. More than a dozen drivers in Oregon weren't so lucky last week.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, Fox 5

item.title: A man called 911 for help during a home invasion. Las Vegas police fatally shot him
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — A Las Vegas man called for police help during a home invasion before an officer fatally shot him, according to authorities and 911 calls. Brandon Durham called 911 early Nov.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5
